programmes.show_description: A look at how the amazing, and massive, decorations on Mardi Gras carnival floats are created, how rock candy is twisted together, and a look at how dentures are made.
How do they harvest and treat the world's most expensive tea? Plus, how does the Siekierki power plant of Warsaw, Poland heat half the city's homes?
Jag XK8. Mike is looking for a British classic and finds an XK8 Jaguar, but it's not in good shape. Will Edd be able to fix it up within budget or is this project about to stall?
Cobra. Mike tracks down a racy Gardner Douglas Mark 3 AC Cobra with only 800 miles on the clock. But Edd needs to make it green to make it legal; will he need to buy a new engine?
Model T Ford. Henry and Fuzz arrive at Cheltenham and find Andy's vintage Model T Ford, which is in desperate need of restoration. Are they up to the task?
Find out how boiled sweets, polystyrene and metal fences are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope.
Find out how mustard, violins and toilet paper are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A woman asks ceramics conservator Kirsten to repair a plate that bears a photograph of her late mother. Plus, Barry restores an old military compass.
New Beginnings. Drew and Viki buy from new business ventures. They attend the opening day of an antiques fair and visit a brand-new antiques centre.
Country House Antiques. Drew and Al buy from sellers who share their love for country house antiques. They snap up an 18th-century chest of drawers and smokers' bow chairs.
The Poseidon Crew finally get permission to dig for gold in century-old mine shafts. And, the Gold Timers take a gamble on a new lease.
The Dust Devils' operation halts and the hold-up could cost them thousands of dollars. A cyclone causes damage to Shane and Russell's wash plant.
Izzie and Phil travel to Essex to hunt for elusive antique treasures. Will a gamble on some brown furniture prove profitable at the Hertfordshire auction?
Angus is excited to clear out a historic property in Long Itchington. Each room is full of interesting items, including vintage furniture and precious jewellery.
A woman asks ceramics conservator Kirsten to repair a plate that bears a photograph of her late mother. Plus, Barry restores an old military compass.
Sam and Henry rescue interesting items from a scrapyard in Devon. The guys find some classic vehicles, including a neglected Jaguar Mark 1.
Sam and Henry visit an incredible collection of Ford vehicles in Worcestershire. But the guys have their sights set on a 1960s Austin A40 Farina.
Vek McGuire. Henry and Fuzz head to London to meet Vek and uncover a tired 1937 James motorcycle. Drawn in by its graceful lines, the guys get to work.
Dave Parkin. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave to acquire his Honda C90 and Gaz-69 Jeep. They're determined to revive both machines and return them to road-ready glory.
Angus and the team clear out a big house in Whixall, Shropshire. They uncover a Victorian desk set, a hanging lantern and Vogue clothing patterns.
Angus is in Newcastle to clear out a home that's full of books. He takes an interest in a Bechstein piano, a scary vase and a silver loving cup.
Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw head to York to hunt for amazing antiques. He tries to build on his earlier success, she's trying to stay in the game.
Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw drive their vintage car through Norfolk and Suffolk hunting for antiques. They unearth Ottoman militaria and more.
Martin. Henry and Fuzz visit Martin in Ipswich. Henry has his eye on a vintage Land Rover and Fuzz finds a miniature scooter, but what will Martin part with?
Roger. Henry and Fuzz explore Roger's garage of buses in Coventry. For former mechanic Fuzz, it's a busman's holiday, for Henry, it's a voyage of discovery.

programmes.show_name: New: Francis Bourgeois & Chris Harris: We Saved A Train
programmes.show_description: Diesel In Distress. Trainspotting superstar Francis takes Chris to the world's biggest train show to recruit him for the epic restoration of diesel royalty.
Model T Ford. Henry and Fuzz arrive at Cheltenham and find Andy's vintage Model T Ford, which is in desperate need of restoration. Are they up to the task?
How do they engineer the latest incarnation of the classic Royal Enfield Continental GT motorbike? Plus, how do 'art detectives' spot a forgery?
How do they blend science and destruction at crash test centres? And how do they piece together the complex mechanism of a pocket sized umbrella?
How do they extract iron ore from one of the most remote mines on the planet? And how do they make chandeliers that are fit for a queen?
How do they forge the blades to crack open oyster shells? Plus, how do scientists in a lab grow the synthetic diamonds of the future?
Jaguar Mark 2. Mike is firing on all cylinders when he tracks down a Jaguar Mark 2. But when Edd examines this iconic classic, will they find the job is bigger than their budget?
Willys Jeep - USA. Mike heads to the USA to find the grandfather of four wheel drives, the Willys Jeep. But will major repairs and high transport costs stop them making a profit?
Sam and Henry rescue interesting items from a scrapyard in Devon. The guys find some classic vehicles, including a neglected Jaguar Mark 1.
Sam and Henry visit an incredible collection of Ford vehicles in Worcestershire. But the guys have their sights set on a 1960s Austin A40 Farina.
Find out how inflatable water toys, couscous and modelling dough are made, as more everyday objects are put under the microscope.
Find out how wind generators, PVC gloves, thermoformed glass and fire engines are made, as more everyday objects put under the microscope.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Steve and Dominic are tasked with repairing a clock that stood for decades above the entrance of a newsagent's. Sonnaz works on a wooden barber's chair.
Lifelong Collectors. Drew Pritchard and Viki Knott travel across the country, visiting incredible collectors who have collections of unusual, rare and historic treasures.
Trusted Hunting Grounds. Drew takes Al on a tour of trusted contacts, finding classic signage in a Kent railway shed, and an African colonial table at a Sussex antiques shop.
The Poseidon Crew unearth two monster nuggets. The Gold Timers battle extreme heat and millions of flies as they desperately try to find gold.
Gold thieves hit the Dust Devils' Siberia lease and steal a fortune in gold. Marcus and Linden persevere through extreme heat to unearth more nuggets.
Izzie and Phil drive through a downpour in Oxfordshire to search of treasures. Will a long-lost saucer or designer brooch prove profitable the Harrogate auction?
The Road To Riches, By Bus Or By Bike. In Sheffield, Angus helps a family find a new loving home for a collection of motorbikes left by their much-missed husband and dad.
Steve and Dominic are tasked with repairing a clock that stood for decades above the entrance of a newsagent's. Sonnaz works on a wooden barber's chair.
Sam and Henry are in Dorset to look around a collection of bikes, cars and tractors. The guys consider buying a pedestrian walk sign and a Yamaha FS1-SE.
Henry and Sam are in Devon to visit a father and daughter who own a shed full of vintage Americana. They immediately take interest in a dusty Chicago police car.
Henry Cole and the team take on a classic 1950s Massey Ferguson combine harvester in one of their biggest restorations yet.
In Scotland, Angus and the team clear out an attic filled with Ferrari memorabilia. How much will this incredible collection make at auction?
In the seaside town of Filey, Angus and the team help a couple clear out their home. Angus sees potential in some vintage toys and boxes of old soap.
Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw are on the final leg of their road trip. With their last auction in Diss, who will make the biggest profit?
James Braxton and Raj Bisram start the first leg of their trip in Bath. They travel to Bristol and Hungerford, before getting to the Isle of Wight.
Theo And Sarah. Henry and Fuzz meet a lifelong biker with unique motorbikes. Fuzz discovers a 1950s step through scooter, but Henry prefers low-capacity sports bikes.
Adam And Dan. Henry and stand-in searcher Guy are in Wiltshire visiting collectors Adam and Dan who have an assortment of trucks, tractors, and rare American cars.
Lost Oils And Hidden Porcelain. Irita finds old Italian paintings and the rarest porcelain she's ever sold, and an aspiring antiques dealer finds out if he has an eye for a bargain.
Time To Let Go. Angus finds thousands of comics that's one man's obsession, but his wife wants them gone. Plus, a man's poor health forces him to give up his passion for timepieces.
How do they make cheddar, the world's most popular cheese? How do they breathe life into Rususci Anne - the plastic woman that teaches CPR?
How do Ford produce the ubiquitous white van, the Transit? Plus, how do they ensure the Driza-bone coat lives up to its name?
